[[File:GazouBoshu.png|240px|thumb|にごり湯]] '''にごり湯'''（にごりゆ）とは、温泉成分の影響で不透明に見える[[温泉]]のこと<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>。
== 概要 == 温泉は湧出すときはは[[無色]][[透明]]であっても、[[地表]]に湧出してから温泉成分の[[酸化]]や、[[圧力]]の変化のために[[成分]]が変化して色がつくこともあり、色付きの[[源泉]]もある。[[硫黄]]や[[カルシウム]]などの含有成分が変化し<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref>、濁り、温泉成分によって様々な色となる。乳白色のものは、[[硫黄]]成分が酸化してできる[[湯の花]]によって着色する。
== 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組] * [http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE] * [http://www.nta.co.jp/omc/tokusyuu/nigoriyu.html 日本旅行OMC]
{{DEFAULTSORT:にこりゆ}} [[category:温泉]]

{{DEFAULTSORT:にこりゆ}} [[category:温泉]]

revision.text:
[[File:141108 Gekkoen Yugetsusanso Arima Onsen Kobe Japan03bs3.jpg|thumb|upright|{{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉}} Kinsen]] '''Nigori-yu''' (にごり湯) refers to {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}} waters that appear opaque due to the influence of hot spring components.<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>
While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|source water|ja|源泉}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}}
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{reflist}}

[[File:141108 Gekkoen Yugetsusanso Arima Onsen Kobe Japan03bs3.jpg|thumb|upright|{{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉}} Kinsen]] '''Nigori-yu''' (にごり湯) refers to {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}} waters that appear opaque due to the influence of hot spring components.<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>
While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|source water|ja|源泉}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.

revision.text:
[[File:141108 Gekkoen Yugetsusanso Arima Onsen Kobe Japan03bs3.jpg|thumb|upright|{{ill|Arima Onsen|ja|有馬温泉}} Kinsen]] '''Nigori-yu''' (にごり湯) refers to {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉}} waters that appear opaque due to the influence of hot spring components.<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>
While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|source water|ja|源泉|1=Source water}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉}}
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{reflist}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Murky Hot Springs}} <!--Murky Hot Springs-->
{{DEFAULTSORT:Murky Hot Springs}} <!--Murky Hot Springs-->
[[sn:Murky Hot Springs]]
[[sn:Murky Hot Springs]] [[ja:にごり湯]]
[[sn:Murky Hot Springs]]

While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|source water|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.

== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Sources === {{reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:にごり湯]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24870922]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:141108 Gekkoen Yugetsusanso Arima Onsen Kobe Japan03bs3.jpg|thumb|upright|[[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Kinsen]] '''Nigori-yu''' (にごり湯) refers to [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] waters that appear opaque due to the influence of hot spring components.<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>
While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/colorless|qq=}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transparent|qq=}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oxidation|qq=}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pressure|qq=}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|Spring Mouth|ja|源泉|qq=draft|lt=Spring Mouths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur|qq=}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calcium|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]]
== References ==
=== Sources === {{reflist}}
{{Onsen}}
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|にごり湯|version=99626300|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|Spring Mouth|ja|源泉|qq=draft|lt=Spring Mouths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur|qq=}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calcium|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:にごり湯]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:にごり湯]] {{Expand Japanese|にごり湯|date=November 2025}} <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:にごり湯]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24870922]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:141108 Gekkoen Yugetsusanso Arima Onsen Kobe Japan03bs3.jpg|thumb|upright|{{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|WD=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen}} Kinsen]] '''Nigori-yu''' (にごり湯) refers to {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} waters that appear opaque due to the influence of hot spring components.<ref>[http://travel.biglobe.ne.jp/onsen/jiten/senmon_05.html BIGLOBE]</ref>
While hot springs may emerge from the ground as {{ill|colorless|en|Colorless|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/colorless|qq=}} and {{ill|transparent|en|Transparency and translucency|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transparent|qq=}}, changes in {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oxidation|qq=}} or {{ill|pressure|en|Pressure|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pressure|qq=}} after reaching the surface can alter their composition and cause coloration. Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|Spring Mouth|ja|源泉|lt=Spring Mouths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur|qq=}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calcium|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}}
== References ==
=== Sources === {{reflist}}
{{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|にごり湯|version=99626300|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--Murky Hot Springs--><!--Murky Hot Springs--><!--Murky Hot Springs-->
[[ja:にごり湯]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q24870922}} [[Category:1000 shortest pages as of Nov 16, 2025]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hot Spring Types]] [[Category:Hot_springs_of_Japan]] [[Category:Murky Hot Springs]] [[Category:onsens]] [[Category:Translated pages on the short pages]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|Spring Mouth|ja|源泉|lt=Spring Mouths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur|qq=}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calcium|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.

Some hot springs also have naturally colored {{ill|Spring Mouth|ja|源泉|lt=Spring Mouths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}. The opacity and coloration are caused by transformations in components such as {{ill|sulfur|en|Sulfur|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur|qq=}} or {{ill|calcium|en|Calcium|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calcium|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.onsen-shinsengumi.com/nigori.html 温泉新選組]</ref> For example, milky white waters are colored by {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}} formed from oxidized sulfur compounds.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}}
